scenario: Jun 1-2: Iran suspended peace talks (The New Arab, Anadolu) — major reversal of MOU deal momentum. US hit Iranian radar and drone installations; Iran retaliated targeting US military base in Kuwait (Semafor, June 1-2). Trump: 'it will all work out well in the end'. Israel-Hezbollah halt hostilities agreed (Jun 1, Al Jazeera). Oil closed up +4% Monday Jun 1 (Reuters). Brent $94.53 (OilPrice.com Jun 2, +2.7% from Friday). Tail 35% (up from 27% on talks suspension + direct military exchange). Barclays warns oil risks 'skewed higher'. Ships sailing dark through Hormuz.

categories.label: Food & Agriculture
categories.supplyChainImpact: Middle East urea and DAP fertilizer exports transit Hormuz, supplying ~30% of Asia's planted-area inputs. Shipping delays of 10-14 days via Cape route raise input costs and threaten planting windows for Vietnam, Thailand, and Indonesia rice belts. Palm oil - Asia's dominant cooking oil - faces surging logistics costs as Malaysia and Indonesia divert vessels.

assets.name: Wheat
assets.narrative: Wheat jumps on talks suspension (Jun 1-2; Brent $94.53 +2.7%; Iran suspends peace talks, US-Iran exchange fire). Tail 35% (up from 27%) — supply chain risk re-pricing on deal collapse probability. Qafco force majeure and Cape rerouting persist until Hormuz signed + mine-cleared. Source: Barchart ZWK26 (est.).

assets.name: Corn
assets.narrative: Corn jumps on crude correlation (Brent $94.53 Jun 2 +2.7%; Iran suspends peace talks). Biofuel demand outlook improves on higher oil. Cape routing disruption unchanged until Hormuz physically reopens. Tail 35%.

assets.name: Soybeans
assets.narrative: Soybeans rise on crude correlation (Brent $94.53 Jun 2; Iran suspends talks). Bunker costs rebounding on talks suspension. Cape routing costs persist until physical Hormuz reopening. Tail 35%.

assets.name: Palm Oil (CPO)
assets.narrative: CPO jumps on crude correlation (Brent $94.53 Jun 2 +2.7%; Iran suspends peace talks). Indonesia B40 mandate structural floor intact. Malaysia supply stable until July per Petronas assurance. Source: Bursa Malaysia BMD (est.).

assets.name: Rice (Thai 25% FOB)
assets.narrative: Rice jumps on food security repricing (Brent $94.53 Jun 2; Iran suspends peace talks — tail 35%). Qafco force majeure persists until deal; planting window pressure acute. ASEAN food security premium expands on talks suspension.

categories.label: Petrochemicals & Plastics
categories.supplyChainImpact: SABIC (Saudi), QAPCO (Qatar), and OCI (Oman) are among the world's largest petrochemical exporters - all ship via Hormuz. SE Asian crackers in Singapore, Thailand, and Malaysia rely on naphtha feedstock sourced from Gulf condensate. Downstream plastics, packaging, and agrochemicals face double squeeze: higher feedstock costs and logistics disruption.

assets.name: Naphtha (CFR Japan)
assets.narrative: Naphtha rises on crude (Brent $94.53 Jun 2 +2.7%; Iran suspends peace talks — talks suspension reverses prior easing). Gulf condensate interdiction persists until Hormuz physically reopens. Tail 35%. Source: ICIS/Platts (est.).

assets.name: Ethylene (CFR NE Asia)
assets.narrative: Ethylene tracks naphtha higher (Jun 1-2 est.; Brent $94.53 +2.7%; Iran suspends peace talks). SABIC/QAPCO shipping bottlenecks persist until mine-clearing. Tail 35%. Source: ICE/ICIS CFR NE Asia (est.).

assets.name: Methanol (CFR China)
assets.narrative: Methanol rises on crude (Brent $94.53 Jun 2; Iran suspends peace talks). US Iran sanctions waiver expired Apr 19; Qatar force majeure persists; OFAC Economic Fury (May 11) enforcement unchanged. Tail 35%. Source: Investing.com CFR China (est.).

categories.label: Fertilizers
categories.supplyChainImpact: The Strait of Hormuz is the critical export artery for Middle East fertilizers: Saudi Arabia, Qatar, Oman, and UAE together account for ~18% of global urea supply and ~12% of DAP. QatarEnergy (Qafco) - world's largest urea exporter - declared force majeure on Mar 2. Iran is also a significant potash and urea producer. Asian planting windows create an acute near-term demand cliff.

assets.name: Urea (FOB Middle East)
assets.narrative: Urea jumps on talks suspension (Jun 1-2; Brent $94.53 +2.7%; Iran suspends peace talks — Qafco fm lifting delayed indefinitely). Tail 35% (up from 27%) supports elevated fertilizer pricing. OFAC Economic Fury enforcement unchanged. Source: DTN, Argus Fertilizers (est.).

assets.name: DAP (FOB US Gulf)
assets.narrative: DAP rises on talks suspension (Jun 1-2; Brent $94.53 +2.7%). Saudi Ma'aden shipping disruption persists until Hormuz physically reopens. Wet-season planting window in Philippines and Vietnam acute. Tail 35%.

assets.name: Potash (MOP CFR SE Asia)
assets.narrative: Potash freight rises on oil (Brent $94.53 Jun 2; Iran suspends peace talks). Cape rerouting persists until Hormuz mine-cleared. Palm oil plantations: input cost pressure rising again. Tail 35%.

categories.label: Shipping & Freight
categories.supplyChainImpact: P&I club war risk coverage withdrawal (effective March 5) triggered mass tanker re-routing via Cape of Good Hope, adding 10-14 transit days and absorbing ~8% of global VLCC fleet capacity. Container lines have activated war risk surcharges and re-routed services, extending Asia-Europe transit times. Bunker fuel costs elevated by crude spike compound freight economics.

assets.name: Baltic Dry Index
assets.narrative: BDI rises on talks suspension (Jun 1-2; Brent $94.53 +2.7%). Iran suspends peace talks extends P&I suspension and Cape rerouting indefinitely. Mine-clearing ~6 months from any deal. Source: Baltic Exchange via Hellenic Shipping News (est.).

assets.name: VLCC AG-Asia Rate
assets.narrative: VLCC rates rebound on talks suspension (Jun 1-2; Brent $94.53 +2.7%). Iran suspends peace talks — physical reopening timeline extended indefinitely. Mine-clearing ~6 months. P&I restoration conditional. Tail 35% supports elevated rate floor. Source: Seatrade Maritime, Lloyd's List (est.).

assets.name: Container Freight (SCFI)
assets.narrative: SCFI rises on talks suspension (pub pending; updated est. on talks suspension + oil spike). Iran suspends peace talks — Cape rerouting premium extends indefinitely. Mine-clearing ~6 months regardless. Tail 35%. Source: Shanghai Shipping Exchange (weekly, est. intra-week move).
